visa_types.name: Short Stay
visa_types.overview:
If you wish to stay in Denmark for more than three months you must apply for a residence permit. 

                                                            From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit.
                                                        

                                                            The embassy fee should be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)

                                                            The receipt for the payment should be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. Please note that in R/W permit cases there are three fees to be paid:
                                                        
- The fee to either The Danish Immigration Service or The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI),
- The fee to the Danish embassy (using the link
https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
) and
- The fee to the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS).”
visa_types.full_text:
If you wish to stay in Denmark for more than three months you must apply for a residence permit. 

                                                            From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit.
                                                        

                                                            The embassy fee should be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)

                                                            The receipt for the payment should be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. Please note that in R/W permit cases there are three fees to be paid:
                                                        
- The fee to either The Danish Immigration Service or The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI),
- The fee to the Danish embassy (using the link
https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
) and
- The fee to the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS).”

visa_types.name: Business / Official visit
visa_types.documents_required:
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
If you are applying under the Red Carpet please print out the Checklist and prepare the required
                                                                        documents accordingly.
Note:

- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
- Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Study/Sport/Cultural Visit]
Please click here  (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)to check visa fee
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
VFS Global Service Fee:
Service charge applicable when applying at the Denmark Visa Application Centre:

[Table:   Study/Sport/Cultural Visit]
*Please note that the service fee is inclusive of tax
Applicants may choose to pay at the Visa Application Centre using Credit/Debit Card or deposit the applicable at the below Bank. Original proof of payment must be received with the visa application. The passport number of the applicant must be clearly stated as reference on the proof of payment or a bank deposit slip.
Please pay attention when booking an appointment and when paying the visa fee. There are several options: Denmark Visa Application Centers (VFS Global) in Pretoria, Durban and Cape Town.
Please note Applicants applying outside Pretoria need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 5 (ZAR 95) it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Pretoria back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across South Africa.
Kindly note that a refund is not an option in case of an erroneous payment.
Banking details for all applicants applying at the DENMARK VISA APPLICATION CENTRE in Cape Town / Pretoria / Durban / Johannesburg
DENMARK VISA APPLICATION CENTRE BANKING DETAILS
Name of beneficiary: VFS Visa Processing SA
Bank: Nedbank
Account Number: 1454119535
Branch Code: 730020
Reference: Passport Number of the applicant to be used
Click for the price list of additional services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/zaf/en/dnk/premium-services)
Special note for all applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre:
- The applicable visa fees in ZAR depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications
- The visa fees need to be paid via debit / credit card at the Visa Application Centre or alternatively via cash deposit at any Nedbank Bank
- We only accept VFS Service fee and Value added service at our office.
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.
visa_types.processing_time:
Due to a current high demand in visa applications for Denmark and Iceland, applicants must expect a longer processing time. The Consulate’s processing time in visa cases is currently approximately 15 days (subject to change). Also please note that business applications are prioritized.
Please refer to Visa Code  Art.23 for more information.  Click here to
                                                                        access the Visa Codes.
Please note that all applications are processed individually. The Visa Officer may invite you for an interview or request further documentation or information, which can prolong the processing
                                                                        time.
visa_types.forms:
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa
                                                                        fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage ) ‘’
                                                                        .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form 
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the
                                                                        required documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6uf6t4hymWuXxsP1dDvKKY/74ec58ffbc8e6ac0125e76be561abe4f/business_checklist_southafrica_aug_2024_final.pdf) and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of Business. This visa is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of Business. This visa is given for a travel period of less than 90 days.

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Study/Sport/Cultural Visit]
Please click here  (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage/1210)to check visa fee
Note: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the visa application fee online using ApplyVisa.um.dk (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage)
VFS Global Service Fee:
Service charge applicable when applying at the Denmark Visa Application Centre:

[Table:   Study/Sport/Cultural Visit]
*Please note that the service fee is inclusive of tax
Applicants may choose to pay at the Visa Application Centre using Credit/Debit Card or deposit the applicable at the below Bank. Original proof of payment must be received with the visa application. The passport number of the applicant must be clearly stated as reference on the proof of payment or a bank deposit slip.
Please pay attention when booking an appointment and when paying the visa fee. There are several options: Denmark Visa Application Centers (VFS Global) in Pretoria, Durban and Cape Town.
Please note Applicants applying outside Pretoria need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 5 (ZAR 95) it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Pretoria back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across South Africa.
Kindly note that a refund is not an option in case of an erroneous payment.
Banking details for all applicants applying at the DENMARK VISA APPLICATION CENTRE in Cape Town / Pretoria / Durban / Johannesburg
DENMARK VISA APPLICATION CENTRE BANKING DETAILS
Name of beneficiary: VFS Visa Processing SA
Bank: Nedbank
Account Number: 1454119535
Branch Code: 730020
Reference: Passport Number of the applicant to be used
Click for the price list of additional services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/zaf/en/dnk/premium-services)
Special note for all applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre:
- The applicable visa fees in ZAR depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications
- The visa fees need to be paid via debit / credit card at the Visa Application Centre or alternatively via cash deposit at any Nedbank Bank
- We only accept VFS Service fee and Value added service at our office.
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
If you are applying under the Red Carpet please print out the Checklist and prepare the required
                                                                        documents accordingly.
Note:

- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
- Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.

## Photo Specifications

- Applicants are no longer required to submit physical photo if they visit the VAC and enrol their biometrics.
- Size of photograph for visa applications
- After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the
                                                                            head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
- The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
- Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above
                                                                            format.
- Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
- For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website
https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
Due to a current high demand in visa applications for Denmark and Iceland, applicants must expect a longer processing time. The Consulate’s processing time in visa cases is currently approximately 15 days (subject to change). Also please note that business applications are prioritized.
Please refer to Visa Code  Art.23 for more information.  Click here to
                                                                        access the Visa Codes.
Please note that all applications are processed individually. The Visa Officer may invite you for an interview or request further documentation or information, which can prolong the processing
                                                                        time.

## Download Forms
Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa
                                                                        fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage ) ‘’
                                                                        .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form 
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the
                                                                        required documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6uf6t4hymWuXxsP1dDvKKY/74ec58ffbc8e6ac0125e76be561abe4f/business_checklist_southafrica_aug_2024_final.pdf) and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.

## Photo Specifications

Physical photographs will however be applicable to applicants who submit postal applications as per the postal application process.
Size of photograph for visa applications

                                                                        should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.

visa_types.name: Long-term Stay (More Than 90 Days)
visa_types.overview:
If you wish to stay in Denmark for more than three months you must apply for a residence permit. 

                                                            From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing online payment of the embassy fee for residence or work permit. 

                                                            The embassy fee should be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)

                                                            The receipt for the payment should be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS)  (http://www.vfsglobal-denmark.com/SouthAfrica/Schedule-an-Appointment.html)when
                                                            you hand in your passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. Please note that in R/W permit cases there are three fees to be paid: 
- The fee to either The Danish Immigration Service or The Danish Agency for International Recruitment and Integration (SIRI),
- The fee to the Danish embassy (using the link
https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
) and
- The fee to the Danish
Visa Application Centre (VFS) (http://www.vfsglobal-denmark.com/SouthAfrica/visa_fees_at_glance.html)
.

*Please note that the service fee is inclusive of tax
Applicants may choose to pay at the Visa Application Centre using Credit/Debit Card or deposit the applicable at the below Bank. Original proof of payment must be received with the visa application. The passport number of the applicant must be clearly stated as reference on the proof of payment or a bank deposit slip.
Please pay attention when booking an appointment and when paying the visa fee. There are several options: Denmark Visa Application Centers (VFS Global) in Pretoria, Durban and Cape Town.
Please note Applicants applying outside Pretoria need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 5 (ZAR 95) it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Pretoria back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across South Africa.
Kindly note that a refund is not an option in case of an erroneous payment.
Banking details for all applicants applying at the DENMARK VISA APPLICATION CENTRE in Cape Town / Pretoria / Durban / Johannesburg
DENMARK VISA APPLICATION CENTRE BANKING DETAILS
Name of beneficiary: VFS Visa Processing SA
Bank: Nedbank
Account Number: 1454119535
Branch Code: 730020
Reference: Passport Number of the applicant to be used
Click for the price list of additional services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/zaf/en/dnk/premium-services)
Special note for all applicants applying at the Visa Application Centre:
- The applicable visa fees in ZAR depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications
- The visa fees need to be paid via debit / credit card at the Visa Application Centre or alternatively via cash deposit at any Nedbank Bank
- We only accept VFS Service fee and Value added service at our office.
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Note:

- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
- Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.

## Photo Specifications

- Applicants are no longer required to submit physical photo if they visit the VAC and enrol their biometrics.
- Size of photograph for visa applications
- After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the
                                                                            head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
- The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
- Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above
                                                                            format.
- Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
- For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website
https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
Due to a current high demand in visa applications for Denmark and Iceland, applicants must expect a longer processing time. The Consulate’s processing time in visa cases is currently approximately 15 days (subject to change). Also please note that business applications are prioritized.
Please refer to Visa Code  Art.23 for more information.  Click here to
                                                                        access the Visa Codes.
Please note that all applications are processed individually. The Visa Officer may invite you for an interview or request further documentation or information, which can prolong the processing
                                                                        time.

## Download Forms
- Register your visa application online and book an appointment with VFS
"NEW MANDATORY ONLINE VISA APPLICATION: From the 01 July 2019, you must fill in your visa application and pay the application fee online. After you have applied and paid the visa
                                                                        fee online, you must print the cover letter and payment receipt from ApplyVisa (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage ) ‘’
                                                                        .
Click here (https://applyvisa.um.dk/NVP.App/frontpage) to fill in your visa application form 
After completing the visa application form and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment to submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the
                                                                        required documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1tbWiVN53TDFQvAEi1Y4VF/e04465329eb7c7b0c931b51bca4d24b7/study-sports-checklist.pdf) and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.

visa_types.name: Red carpet
visa_types.documents_required:
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly. 
Here you can find further information about the Red Carpet agreement, how you can apply for it, who can apply for it, as well as the terms and conditions regarding the Red Carpet Visa agreement. 
Please see this link for further information: http://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/company-accredition/ (http://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/company-accredition/)
Contact the relevant Danish mission, if you are interested in obtaining a Red Carpet agreement.
visa_types.overview:
The Red Carpet Program introduces simplified procedures for obtaining Danish Schengen visas for applicants who travel regularly to Denmark for business purposes. The aim is to assist those companies,
                                                        organizations, NGO’s etc. that have business relations with a Danish company or organization.
The Red Carpet Program is open to private and public companies or organizations provided they meet the requirements mentioned on the local Danish embassy’s website. The accreditation of a company
                                                        or organization must have taken place before an employee/associate can benefit from the Red Carpet Program.
visa_types.full_text:
The Red Carpet Program introduces simplified procedures for obtaining Danish Schengen visas for applicants who travel regularly to Denmark for business purposes. The aim is to assist those companies,
                                                        organizations, NGO’s etc. that have business relations with a Danish company or organization.
The Red Carpet Program is open to private and public companies or organizations provided they meet the requirements mentioned on the local Danish embassy’s website. The accreditation of a company
                                                        or organization must have taken place before an employee/associate can benefit from the Red Carpet Program. 

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly. 
Here you can find further information about the Red Carpet agreement, how you can apply for it, who can apply for it, as well as the terms and conditions regarding the Red Carpet Visa agreement. 
Please see this link for further information: http://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/company-accredition/ (http://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/how-to-apply-for-a-visa/company-accredition/)
Contact the relevant Danish mission, if you are interested in obtaining a Red Carpet agreement.

## Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. This visa is given for a travel period of less than
                                                                        90 days.

## Visa Fees

Please note Applicants applying outside Pretoria need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 5 (ZAR 95) it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Pretoria back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across South Africa.

## Documents Required
Please print out this Checklist and prepare the required documents accordingly.
Note:

- Kindly make sure that your application contains sufficient, correct and adequate information. All information must be correct and reflect your planned trip.
- In case any document is missing on submission date, additional documents can be submitted only by applicants themselves at VFS within 5 business days.
- Applicant can be asked for additional documents or may be called for an interview if deemed necessary by the Embassy.
- Applicants are no longer required to submit a copy of their current passport. This requirement is applicable only for previous/old passports, where relevant.

## Photo Specifications

- Applicants are no longer required to submit physical photo if they visit the VAC and enrol their biometrics.

- Size of photograph for visa applications
- After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the
                                                                            head should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
- The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
- Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above
                                                                            format.
- Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
- For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website
https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
Due to a current high demand in visa applications for Denmark and Iceland, applicants must expect a longer processing time. The Consulate’s processing time in visa cases is currently approximately 15 days (subject to change). Also please note that business applications are prioritized.

visa_types.name: Work And Residence Permit
visa_types.documents_required:
Mandatory documents for online applications and applications submitted by the company in Denmark
- A print of the confirmation that the application was submitted online (the confirmation has information about case order ID)
- Valid original travel document
- Other required supporting documents followed by rightfully translated versions according to the list covering the specific application form
Mandatory documents for applications submitted by post and applications submitted by the company in Denmark
- A copy of the first three pages of the application form which was forwarded by regular postage mail to DIS/SIRI
- Valid original travel document
- Other required supporting documents followed by rightfully translated versions according to the list covering the specific application form
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Work And Residence Permit]

Note: From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee
                                                                        for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/).The
                                                                        receipt for the payment must be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. The embassy fee
                                                                        and the fee to the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) must be paid in addition to the fee to The Danish Immigration Service. 

[Work and Residence Permit]
Please Note:

- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Please note Applicants applying outside Pretoria need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 5 (ZAR 95) it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Pretoria back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across South Africa.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
- The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in Rands.
visa_types.processing_time: The Immigration Service has set maximum expected processing times for residence permit applications, please visit The Danish Immigration Service website (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-are-waiting-for-an-answer) for more information.
visa_types.forms:
To download or access online application forms, please click here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply).
From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee (http://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/payment-for-services/) for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
After completing the visa application form (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Applying) and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment
                                                                        to submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
For information of residence and work permits including application form, please visit www.newtodenmark.dk. (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For information of residence and work permits including application form, please visit www.newtodenmark.dk. (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB)

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Work And Residence Permit]

Note: From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee
                                                                        for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/).The
                                                                        receipt for the payment must be submitted at the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) when you hand in your passport, relevant documentation and supply biometrics. The embassy fee
                                                                        and the fee to the Danish Visa Application Centre (VFS) must be paid in addition to the fee to The Danish Immigration Service. 

[Work and Residence Permit]
Please Note:

- The applicable VFS service fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change.
- The service fee is inclusive VAT and is non-refundable.
- Please note Applicants applying outside Pretoria need to pay mandatory courier charges which is Euro 5 (ZAR 95) it includes one-way courier charges pertaining to the transfer of visa applications from Pretoria back to regional VFS Visa Application Centers across South Africa.
- Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost. Please see more detail from Additional Services tab.
- The service fee and additional services at The Visa Application Centre are to be paid only in Rands.

## Documents Required
Mandatory documents for online applications and applications submitted by the company in Denmark
- A print of the confirmation that the application was submitted online (the confirmation has information about case order ID)
- Valid original travel document
- Other required supporting documents followed by rightfully translated versions according to the list covering the specific application form
Mandatory documents for applications submitted by post and applications submitted by the company in Denmark
- A copy of the first three pages of the application form which was forwarded by regular postage mail to DIS/SIRI
- Valid original travel document
- Other required supporting documents followed by rightfully translated versions according to the list covering the specific application form

## Photo Specifications
Size of photograph for visa applications
After the instalment of a new system for scanning biometrics, including photographs for visa application, it is now possible to lodge a photograph of size 35 x 45 mm. Length of the head
                                                                        should not exceed 30 to 36 mm in length from chin to top of hair.
The photograph must still be in color with bright background.
Danish Visa Application Centre has been instructed by Danish mission to inform applicants that the visa application is not acceptable if the photograph does not comply with the above
                                                                        format.
Applicants may therefore obtain a new photograph that meets all requirements before The Danish Visa Application Centre can receive the application.
For further information see the print on the information board at The Danish Visa Application Centre or visit website https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto (https://politi.dk/pas/krav-til-pas-og-koerekortfoto)

## Processing Time
The Immigration Service has set maximum expected processing times for residence permit applications, please visit The Danish Immigration Service website (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-are-waiting-for-an-answer) for more information.

## Download Forms
To download or access online application forms, please click here (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/You-want-to-apply).
From 01 July 2019, The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark including all Danish diplomatic missions are introducing mandatory online payment of the embassy fee (http://um.dk/en/travel-and-residence/payment-for-services/) for residence or work permit. The embassy fee must be paid online using this link https://dys.um.dk/permit/ (https://dys.um.dk/permit/)
After completing the visa application form (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-GB/Applying) and paying visa fee online, you must book an appointment
                                                                        to submit your documents at VFS by clicking here. Make sure to bring along all the required documents and hand in supporting documents at the Visa Application Centre.
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Denmark visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
